meta_title: For Freelancers: project management to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Project Management to Fix Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Master project management to overcome debugging bottlenecks. An intermediate guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a code problem; it's a structural one. By implementing rigorous project management, you isolate failure points, maintain modular state, and leverage AI agents like Claude Code to automate diagnostics. This system transforms chaotic troubleshooting into a predictable, repeatable process that saves hours of billable time per project.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of cryptic stack traces, the client’s deadline is breathing down your neck, and the logic you wrote yesterday has suddenly become a black box. The 'can't debug' syndrome isn't a lack of talent; it’s a symptom of unmanaged complexity. When your codebase becomes a tangle of side effects, your brain reaches its cognitive limit.

But what if debugging wasn't an act of desperate detective work? By shifting your workflow to a project-managed architecture, you turn your code into a navigable map. We’ll use Antigravity to structure your logic and Claude Code to act as your tireless pair-programmer, turning debugging from a painful chore into a strategic, automated routine.

steps.title: Architectural Decoupling
steps.description: Isolate your business logic from external dependencies to prevent 'spaghetti debugging'. This makes individual modules testable in isolation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this module into a pure function with no external state dependencies. Ensure inputs and outputs are strictly typed for easier debugging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'decouple business logic from UI components'

steps.title: Implement Observability Hooks
steps.description: Inject structured logging at entry and exit points of your functions to track data flow without manual breakpoints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Insert structured log points at every function boundary that output the state of arguments and return values in JSON format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'add structured logging to all service functions'

steps.title: Automated Regression Testing
steps.description: Use AI to generate unit tests that cover the specific bug you just fixed, ensuring it never returns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for this module that covers edge cases and confirms the fix for the reported bug.

steps.title: Dependency Graph Analysis
steps.description: Map out your module dependencies to identify circular references that often cause unpredictable runtime errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and generate a dependency graph. Identify any circular imports or tight coupling that could impede debugging.

steps.title: AI-Driven Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Feed your error logs and project structure into Claude Code to get a high-level diagnosis before you touch the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review these error logs and the associated module file. Identify the likely root cause and suggest a refactor strategy.

pro_tips: Treat your error logs as data assets; use them to train your own local patterns for common project pitfalls.
Always use a 'debug-only' environment variable to toggle verbose logging so production performance remains pristine.
When using Claude Code, summarize the specific file context before asking for a fix to reduce token waste and improve accuracy.
If a bug persists, force a 'clean build' by clearing your node_modules or equivalent cache; phantom binaries are a silent killer.
Maintain a 'Bug-Fix Log' markdown file in your repo; it acts as a knowledge base that AI can reference to solve recurring client issues.
content.next_steps: Once you have stabilized your debugging workflow, move on to implementing CI/CD pipelines that automatically run your test suite on every commit to ensure your project management remains proactive rather than reactive.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start commanding it with a professional, AI-augmented workflow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
